opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My hands are swollen like sausages, the pain is relentless, and I'm too exhausted to even open a bottle of water.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My hands flared badly over two days after I ran out of my anti-inflammatory — I can't grip or open anything and I'm on my own.

symptom_narrative.character: deep aching, swollen 'sausage' fingers and wrists, prolonged morning stiffness
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: both wrists, knuckles and finger joints, symmetric
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: stiff for over 3 hours each morning; flared after stopping my medication
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: my anti-inflammatory usually helps a lot, but I ran out
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: profound tiredness and I feel a bit feverish
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have suspected seronegative inflammatory arthritis and this flared when I ran out of meds

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I live alone and can't open medication bottles or prepare food tonight

communication_style.typical_message_style: clear, focused on regaining hand function and getting medication

objective_data_offered.value: both hands and wrists are symmetrically swollen and warm; no red streaks up my arms
I can't grip or open bottles
